edge_hypothesis: ema_rsi_atr achieved PF=1.201 at ~4x TP but with catastrophic 69% DD. The advisory identified extreme R:R as the system's universal edge amplifier (MACD went from PF=1.347 at 3.5x to PF=1.712 at 7x). The DD problem is NOT the vol expansion gate — it's the tight R:R. When ATR > ATR_MA triggers entry during high-volatility periods, the absolute stop distance (in $) is large, but so is the potential move. At 2x TP, the stop is hit before the trend develops. At 7-9x TP, the high-volatility entry becomes an ADVANTAGE: the trend has room to run, and the vol expansion ensures the move is large enough to reach extreme TPs. The EMA9/EMA21 crossover + RSI confirmation (40-70 band) provides a quality momentum signal — not as strong as MACD zero-cross, but with MORE frequent entries (195 trades vs 161). If the PF-vs-R:R relationship holds for ema_rsi_atr as it did for MACD, PF should increase from 1.20 to 1.3-1.5 at 7x TP while DD should DECREASE because winners are now 7x risk instead of 2x, meaning fewer consecutive losers are needed to recover. Regime: primarily trending+transitional (ema_rsi_atr showed PF=1.20 trending, PF=1.51 transitional in prior tests). Invalidation: if PF decreases from 4x to 7x TP, the ema_rsi_atr signal quality is too low for extreme tail harvesting — the EMA crossover produces too many false signals that get stopped out before reaching 7x ATR.

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_tail_runner_7to1
variants.description: EMA9/EMA21 crossover + RSI 35-75 + vol expansion gate on ETH/4h with 7:1 R:R. Applies the proven tail harvesting principle to the ema_rsi_atr template. Wider RSI band (35-75 vs default 40-70) allows more entries — the extreme R:R handles noise through position sizing rather than entry filtering. 2.0x ATR stop meets the STOP_FLOOR mandate. At 14.0x ATR TP, this is the same R:R that made MACD achieve its best-ever PF. Expected win rate ~12-16%, but each winner = 7x risk.
entry_long: EMA_9 > EMA_21 (short-term momentum above medium-term, bullish alignment).
RSI_14 between 35 and 75 (not oversold, not overbought — momentum sweet spot).
ATR_14 > 20-bar ATR MA (volatility expansion gate — trend is moving).
entry_short: EMA_9 < EMA_21 (short-term momentum below medium-term, bearish alignment).
RSI_14 between 25 and 65 (inverse momentum sweet spot).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0, ema_rsi_atr signal quality is too low for 7x tail harvesting — revert to 4x TP
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 / EMA_21 crossover — momentum alignment signal
RSI_14 band 35-75 — wider than default to allow more entries at extreme R:R
ATR_14 vol expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R tail harvesting

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_tight_momentum_9to1
variants.description: EMA9/EMA21 crossover + RSI 45-65 (tight band) + vol expansion gate on ETH/4h with 9:1 R:R. The opposite approach: instead of widening RSI to get more entries, TIGHTEN RSI to 45-65 for higher signal quality. RSI between 45-65 means momentum is moderate — not overbought, not oversold, not even approaching extremes. This captures the 'momentum launch zone' where price has room to run in either direction. Fewer entries but each entry should have higher probability of reaching the extreme 18x ATR TP. 2.0x stop. Expected win rate ~8-10% but each winner = 9x risk.
entry_long: RSI_14 between 45 and 65 (moderate momentum — not extended, room to run).
entry_short: RSI_14 between 35 and 55 (inverse moderate momentum zone).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (18.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 AND trade count < 50, signal is too selective for 9:1 — widen RSI band
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 18.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: RSI_14 band 45-65 — tight 'launch zone' for high-quality entries only
ATR_14 vol expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 18.0x TP — 9:1 R:R extreme tail harvesting

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_1h_vol_runner_6to1
variants.description: EMA9/EMA21 crossover + RSI 35-70 + vol expansion gate on ETH/1h with 6:1 R:R. The 1h timeframe variant. Previous ema_rsi_atr tests on 1h showed PF=1.142 with 163 trades — more trade volume than 4h. At 6:1 R:R on 1h, we test whether the increased bar count + moderate extreme R:R can produce both strong PF and manageable DD. The 1h timeframe captures intraday momentum shifts that 4h misses. 6:1 is conservative relative to the other variants but proven as the sweet spot for Supertrend (PF=1.22 ACCEPT).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < PF at 4x on 1h (1.142), the ema_rsi_atr signal degrades at wider TPs on 1h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: RSI_14 band 35-70 — standard band, 1h timeframe for more trades
ATR_14 vol expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 12.0x TP — 6:1 R:R proven sweet spot
